body:
算了，保留吧，默认配置冗余多一点比较好，删除 expectIPs 同时满足以下条件可能导致访问缓慢：

1. X 域名不在 `geosite:cn; 中
2. 用户设置直连 X 域名
3. 用户没有添加 X 到 223.5.5.5 的 domains 中
4. X 只有国内 IP
5. X 在国内有多个 CDN
6. X 的不同 CDN 在不同网络环境下差距大
7. 用户设置 Outbound Freedom domainStrategy 为 UseIP

会导致用户直连到 X 的某离远程节点较近的 CDN，而不是离用户最近的 CDN。

~~虽然触发条件比较多，触发比较困难~~

body:
> For v2rayng, if possible, apply this pr.

@Pk-web6936 v2rayng 是有什么问题吗，起码这个问题不存在吧，路由规则会直接并入到 dns 规则中

1. **用户明确设置为代理的域名**：

   ```
   DNS 查询顺序：1.1.1.1 → 1.1.1.1（fallback）
   ```

2. **用户明确设置为直连的域名**：

   ```
   DNS 查询顺序：223.5.5.5 → 1.1.1.1（回退）→ 1.1.1.1
   ```

3. **用户未指定处理方式的域名**：

   ```
   DNS 查询顺序：1.1.1.1（回退） → 1.1.1.1
   ```

不过可以考虑为 `remoteDns` 加入 `skipFallback = true`，因为二次 1.1.1.1 查询

https://github.com/2dust/v2rayNG/blob/02e53ced50efd0654b52cfda749ae958eacf2870/V2rayNG/app/src/main/java/com/v2ray/ang/handler/V2rayConfigManager.kt#L450

The virtual subscription proxy-chain node hard-codes `CoreType = node.CoreType ?? ECoreType.Xray`. ```

## Pull request overview This PR fixes sing-box “hosts” matching behavior so that non-full-match host keys (e.g., `keyword:example.com`) are handled via generated DNS rules instead of being placed into the `hosts` server predefined map, aligning behavior with sing-box domain matching semantics. **Changes:** - Build a dedicated `resolve` routing rule from hosts entries by parsing v2ray-style domain prefixes into the appropriate sing-box rule fields. - Restrict `hosts` DNS server predefined entries to full-match domains only; route non-full-match host entries into generated DNS `predefined` rules (including IP answers / CNAME / rcode). - Add parsing support for the `dotless:` domain prefix. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/Singbox/SingboxRoutingService.cs | Parses hosts keys into correct sing-box routing match fields when generating the hosts-related `resolve` rule. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/Singbox/SingboxDnsService.cs | Ensures only full-match keys go into the `hosts` server; generates DNS rules for keyword/suffix/regex/etc. hosts mappings. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).

body: The conversion from `domain_keyword` to `domain` is currently gated by `!host.StartsWith("keyword:")`, which will also treat `dotless:` (and any future prefixes that map into `domain_keyword`) as a full match. If the intent is to convert only *unprefixed* domains (so `example.com` behaves like `full:example.com`), consider checking for the absence of a prefix (e.g., no `:` before converting) instead of only excluding `keyword:`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Same issue as in routing: converting `domain_keyword` to `domain` based on `!kvp.Key.StartsWith("keyword:")` will also convert `dotless:` into a full match. If the goal is to allow only unprefixed domains / `full:` here, gate the conversion on “no prefix present” (e.g., no `:`) rather than “not keyword”.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This `domain_keyword` → `domain` conversion is triggered for anything that isn’t explicitly `keyword:`. That means `dotless:` (and any other `domain_keyword`-based prefix) will be rewritten into a full match, changing its semantics. Consider converting only when the original key has no prefix (i.e., the “hosts-style” bare domain case).

body: ## Pull request overview 该 PR 旨在修复 sing-box 在 TUN 模式下与本地 relay（到 Xray shadowsocks relay）交互时的 mux 问题，并尝试改进 Windows 下提权重启相关流程的一致性。 **Changes:** - 修正 sing-box selector/outbound tag 收集逻辑，使用 `baseTagName` 作为前缀匹配基准。 - 针对 TUN 合成的本地 relay shadowsocks 出站，禁用 sing-box mux，避免本地 relay 流量被包装成 `sp.mux.sing-box.arpa`。 - 将 `ProcUtils.RebootAsAdmin` 调整为返回 `bool`，以便调用方区分启动成功/失败（例如 UAC 取消）。 ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/ViewModels/StatusBarViewModel.cs | 当前 diff 仅有空行变更，但该方法仍存在与 PR 描述不一致的提权/保存状态流程问题（见评论）。 | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/Singbox/SingboxOutboundService.cs | 修复 selector tag 前缀筛选逻辑；并为 TUN relay 出站禁用 mux。 | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Common/ProcUtils.cs | `RebootAsAdmin` 改为返回 `bool`，为上层处理 UAC 取消/失败提供能力，但需要配套调用方逻辑。 | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `RebootAsAdmin` now returns a `bool`, but the current pattern in the codebase (e.g., `AppManager.RebootAsAdmin`) still calls it without checking whether the elevated process was actually started. On Windows, UAC cancel typically results in a thrown exception, so `RebootAsAdmin` will return `false` and callers should *not* proceed to exit/restart in that case; instead they should keep/restore the previous UI/config state. Suggest: propagate this return value to the caller and gate the shutdown/restart logic on success (and optionally treat Win32 error 1223 as a non-error log).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In the Windows non-admin elevation path inside `DoEnableTun`, the method returns early *before* reaching `SaveConfig`, and earlier code in the method sets `_config.TunModeItem.EnableTun = false` before calling `RebootAsAdmin`. This contradicts the PR description (preserve `EnableTun=true` across elevation, roll back only on UAC cancel) and is also the root cause of the elevated instance not enabling TUN. Consider persisting `EnableTun=true` before triggering elevation, and using the elevation result to decide whether to roll back and publish `ReloadRequested`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

`RELEASE_TAG` is computed using `case(...)`. If `case` isn’t a supported GitHub Actions expression function, the `deb`/`rpm` jobs will fail at evaluation time. Prefer supported patterns (e.g., `inputs.release_tag != '' && inputs.release_tag || github.ref_name`) or a `fromJSON` mapping if needed.

```

body: This workflow also runs on `push`, but the `release-zip` job gates on `inputs.release_tag`. On non-`workflow_dispatch` events the `inputs` context may be unset/empty, which can make this condition behave unexpectedly (including trying to upload a release with an empty tag). Safer options are to gate on `github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.release_tag != ''`, or switch back to `github.event.inputs.release_tag` for the condition.

body: I have some concerns about using SourceGear.sqlite3. It may introduce a dependency on SourceGear and reduce long-term flexibility.
author: xujiegb

body:
[xujiegb](https://github.com/xujiegb)

The SourceGear.sqlite3 package is not a vendor-lock-in dependency. It is the renamed SQLitePCLRaw.lib.e_sqlite3 package, maintained by Eric Sink (the author of SQLitePCL.raw and a long-time SQLite maintainer). The publicly available version on nuget.org is the standard, free distribution. The renamed name simply reflects the publisher's company, SourceGear LLC.

Reference: the official 3.0 migration guide at [github.com/ericsink/SQLitePCL.raw/blob/main/v3.md](https://github.com/ericsink/SQLitePCL.raw/blob/main/v3.md) explicitly recommends this exact pair (SQLitePCLRaw.config.e_sqlite3 + SourceGear.sqlite3) when one wants explicit control over the SQLite native version, with the rationale: "Going forward, when you want to update to a new build of SQLite, you will only need to update the reference to the SourceGear.sqlite3 package." This is exactly what this PR does.

The paid feed at nuget.sourcegear.com is a separate offering for accelerated SQLite builds and SEE/SQLCipher variants — we are not using it.

body: `AddBatchServers` calls `AddBatchServers4InnerUri` unconditionally. Since `AddBatchServers4InnerUri` removes existing subscription servers via `RemoveServersViaSubid` when `isSub` is true, a normal subscription update with no `v2rayn://` entries will delete the whole subscription list after `AddBatchServersCommon` has already imported it (and the method then returns -1 without re-adding anything). Consider only invoking the inner-uri import when `isSub` is false, or refactor so subscription removal happens once at the top-level and each parser only adds without deleting.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `AddBatchServers4InnerUri` adds items using `Add*Server` / `AddServerCommon` with the default `toFile=true`. Because `InnerFmt.Resolve` pre-populates non-empty `IndexId` values, `AddServerCommon` will not auto-assign sort order (it only sets sort when generating a new `IndexId` or when `toFile=false`). This can leave imported items with the default sort value (0) and also performs per-item DB writes. Consider passing `toFile=false` and doing a bulk insert (like `AddBatchServersCommon`), or explicitly setting sort for each imported `IndexId`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In `Resolve`, you compute `trimmedLine = line.Trim()` but the protocol check uses `line.StartsWith(...)` instead of `trimmedLine.StartsWith(...)`. This prevents parsing internal URIs that have leading whitespace (common when copy/pasting lists). Use the trimmed value for the prefix check to match the subsequent call to `ResolveSingle(trimmedLine)`.

## Pull request overview This PR introduces a new **“Final (tail) fragmentation”** option that can be toggled from both WPF and Avalonia settings UI, persisted in configuration, and applied during core config generation (V2Ray and sing-box) to enable fragmentation behavior at the “final landing” stage. **Changes:** - Adds `EnableFinalFragment` to config + settings view model, and exposes it in WPF/Avalonia option windows with localized strings. - V2Ray: adds `ApplyFinalFragment()` that wraps `proxy*` outbounds with a `freedom` detour outbound applying `finalmask` (TCP fragment + UDP noise). - sing-box: adds routing rules that enable `tls_record_fragment` when the option is enabled. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 13 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/v2rayN/Views/OptionSettingWindow.xaml.cs | Binds the new `EnableFinalFragment` toggle to the view model (WPF). | | v2rayN/v2rayN/Views/OptionSettingWindow.xaml | Adds the new toggle + tip text and shifts subsequent row indices (WPF UI). | | v2rayN/v2rayN.Desktop/Views/OptionSettingWindow.axaml.cs | Binds the new `EnableFinalFragment` toggle to the view model (Avalonia). | | v2rayN/v2rayN.Desktop/Views/OptionSettingWindow.axaml | Adds the new toggle + tip text and shifts subsequent row indices (Avalonia UI). | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/ViewModels/OptionSettingViewModel.cs | Adds reactive property and saves/loads `EnableFinalFragment` from config. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/V2ray/V2rayOutboundService.cs | Refactors fragment mask creation and introduces `ApplyFinalFragment()` to wrap outbounds. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/V2ray/CoreConfigV2rayService.cs | Calls `ApplyFinalFragment()` during V2Ray config generation when enabled. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/Singbox/SingboxRoutingService.cs | Adds `route-options` rules enabling `tls_record_fragment` when enabled. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Resx/ResUI.zh-Hans.resx | Adds Chinese UI strings for the new setting. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Resx/ResUI.resx | Adds English UI strings for the new setting. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Resx/ResUI.Designer.cs | Updates generated resource accessors for the new strings. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Models/CoreConfigs/V2rayConfig.cs | Adds `tcp` to `Finalmask4Ray` model to support serialization. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Models/CoreConfigs/SingboxConfig.cs | Adds `tls_record_fragment` field to sing-box rule model. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Models/Configs/ConfigItems.cs | Persists the new `EnableFinalFragment` option in `CoreBasicItem`. |

body: `fragmentFreedom` is constructed without initializing required non-nullable fields (`settings`, `mux`, and `streamSettings.network/security`). Because serialization ignores nulls, the generated outbound JSON may omit required fields and can produce invalid V2Ray config or cause NREs when the object is later accessed.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using `Replace(Global.ProxyTag, ...)` can rewrite multiple occurrences if a tag contains "proxy" more than once (e.g., `proxy-1-proxy-foo`), producing unexpected `afterTag` values. Since you only need a deterministic new tag, prefixing is safer.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The non-sniffing branch adds a `route-options` rule with `tls_record_fragment` but no match condition (e.g., `protocol = ["tls"]`). This makes the rule apply to all traffic and is inconsistent with the sniffing-enabled branch; it may also be rejected/ignored by sing-box depending on schema validation.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `EnableFinalFragment` changes the generated client config output, but there are no tests covering the enabled/disabled behavior. Since `ServiceLib.Tests` already covers V2ray/Sing-box config generation, adding regression tests here would help prevent future breakage (e.g., ensuring outbounds are wrapped/detoured as expected for V2ray and `tls_record_fragment` appears for sing-box).

body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates v2rayN’s Xray/V2Ray client config generation for **mKCP** to match the newer “mask” configuration format, resolving failures like `unknown config id: header-dtls` reported in the linked discussion thread. **Changes:** - Switch KCP outbound `finalmask.udp` generation to use `type: "mkcp-legacy"` with structured settings instead of legacy mask IDs like `header-dtls` / `mkcp-aes128gcm`. - Extend `MaskSettings4Ray` to support the new KCP mask settings fields (`header`, `value`). - Update `Global.KcpHeaderMaskMap` to map UI header types to the new expected header identifiers (without the `header-` prefix). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/V2ray/V2rayOutboundService.cs | Generates new-style KCP `finalmask.udp` entries using `mkcp-legacy` plus `header`/`value` settings. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Models/CoreConfigs/V2rayConfig.cs | Adds `header` and `value` fields to `MaskSettings4Ray` to serialize the updated KCP mask settings. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Global.cs | Updates KCP header-type mapping to align with new mask header identifiers. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Because `Init()` is started fire-and-forget (`_ = Init();`), there’s a window where bindings/callers can observe `FilterConfigTypes == null`, which contradicts the non-nullable type and can lead to runtime null issues. Initialize it to an empty list (or make it nullable) to match the nullability contract.

body: ## Pull request overview > [!NOTE] > Copilot was unable to run its full agentic suite in this review. Adds macOS support for mitigating TUN DNS bypass when running sing-box with `hijack-dns` routing rules by embedding and invoking a dedicated DNS management script under sudo. **Changes:** - Embed a new `tun_dns_osx_sh` script and expose its embedded-resource name via `Global`. - Detect `hijack-dns` in the sing-box config and run the macOS DNS set/restore flow around sudo-launched core lifecycle. - Ensure DNS restore is attempted on failures and when the sudo process is killed/exits. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/ServiceLib.csproj | Embeds the new macOS TUN DNS helper script as a resource. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Sample/tun_dns_osx_sh | Adds a bash script to set temporary DNS and restore original DNS using a state file. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Manager/CoreAdminManager.cs | Runs the embedded script on macOS when sing-box config contains `hijack-dns`. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Global.cs | Adds a constant for the embedded macOS TUN DNS script resource name. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `CreateLinuxShellFile(..., false)` likely creates a non-executable file (contrast with `run_as_sudo.sh` created with `true`). Since the script is executed directly via `sudo -- scriptPath ...`, lack of execute permission will cause the command to fail. Set the flag to create an executable file (or invoke `/bin/bash scriptPath ...` instead of executing the script path directly).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using a single fixed state file path can cause cross-process interference (e.g., two app instances or rapid restarts overwriting each other’s DNS state, leading to incorrect restores). Prefer a per-instance/per-process state path (e.g., include PID, timestamp, or a GUID) and ensure the corresponding restore uses the same state file.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `RunProcessAsLinuxSudo` now includes macOS-specific behavior (DNS management under macOS). This makes the method name misleading and harder to reason about. Consider renaming to a platform-neutral name (e.g., `RunProcessAsSudo`) or moving macOS-only work into a separate macOS-specific entry point to keep platform responsibilities clear.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Swallowing all exceptions here (including JSON parse errors and IO issues) makes it difficult to diagnose why macOS DNS handling did not activate. Consider at least logging the exception at debug/trace level (or returning an error signal) so misconfigured/invalid configs don’t silently disable the feature.

body: `IsTunActive` is updated here (including being set to `false` on stop), but `AppEvents.TunStateChanged` is only published from `MainWindowViewModel` during load. This can leave the UI in a stale “TUN active” icon/proxy state when the core stops or crashes. Publish `TunStateChanged` whenever `IsTunActive` changes (at minimum in `CoreStop`’s `finally`, and ideally right after computing it in `LoadCore`).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `IsTunActive` is updated here (including being set to `false` on stop), but `AppEvents.TunStateChanged` is only published from `MainWindowViewModel` during load. This can leave the UI in a stale “TUN active” icon/proxy state when the core stops or crashes. Publish `TunStateChanged` whenever `IsTunActive` changes (at minimum in `CoreStop`’s `finally`, and ideally right after computing it in `LoadCore`).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using `async` lambdas directly in `Subscribe` results in an `async void`-like fire-and-forget execution path where exceptions can become unobserved and multiple `TunStateChanged` events can overlap (racing calls to `ChangeSystemProxyAsync`). Consider switching to an Rx-friendly async pattern (e.g., projecting to an observable from the task and using a serialization strategy like `Concat` / `Switch`) and ensure errors are handled/logged.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `_updatingSystemProxySelection` is not protected by `try/finally`. If `SystemProxySelected` (or anything it triggers) throws, the flag may remain `true` permanently, breaking subsequent user-driven updates. Wrap the assignment in `try/finally` to guarantee the flag is reset.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: These flags are written from core-management code paths and read from UI/viewmodel code paths, which are typically on different threads/schedulers. While `bool` reads/writes are atomic, cross-thread visibility/order is not guaranteed. Consider making these thread-safe (e.g., via `volatile` backing fields, `Volatile.Read/Write`, or funneling updates through a single-threaded scheduler/event) to avoid stale reads.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The TUN-aware icon index selection logic is duplicated (similar ternary logic exists in `GetAppIcon` here and in `AvaUtils.GetAppIcon`). This increases the risk of icon selection drifting between platforms/contexts. Consider centralizing icon-index computation in a shared helper (e.g., a small method that takes `sysProxyType`, `isTunActive`, and `isSystemProxySuppressedByTun`) and reuse it in all icon-selection call sites.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This test file relies on `ESysProxyType` and `SysProxyHandler` being available without explicit `using` directives. If this compiles only due to global usings, it can be fragile and harder to read in isolation. Consider adding explicit `using` statements (or fully qualifying) for the production namespaces to make the dependency clear and the file self-contained.
